---
title: "Research & Publications"
description-meta: "Peter Kapec's research areas and publications"
title-block-banner: false
css: custom.css
---

## Vision & Graphics Group

The Vision & Graphics Group is an informal research group at the Institute of Applied Informatics at FIIT STU that is interested in computer vision, computer graphics, visualization and related problem fields.

---

## Research Areas

My research focuses on innovative approaches to understanding and visualizing complex software systems and data structures.

### Primary Research Interests

**Software Visualization in 3D Space**
Developing techniques for visualizing software architecture, dependencies, and evolution in three-dimensional environments to provide better understanding of complex systems.

**Graph Layout & Visualization Algorithms**
Creating efficient algorithms for laying out and visualizing large graphs and networks, including edge bundling techniques to reduce visual clutter.

**Interactive Data Visualization & Visual Data Mining**
Exploring interactive methods for data exploration and mining, enabling users to discover patterns and insights in complex datasets.

**Augmented Reality Applications**
Investigating the use of augmented reality for photorealistic data visualization and software structure representation in real-world contexts.

**Software Evolution & Metrics Visualization**
Analyzing and visualizing how software systems evolve over time, including metrics visualization and program execution dynamics.

**Documentation Generation from Source Code**
Automated approaches to generate and visualize software documentation directly from source code analysis.

---

## Research Projects

Details of current and past research projects will be listed in the [Projects](projects.qmd) section.

---

## Supervised Research

### Thesis Supervision

I have supervised over **40 bachelor's and master's thesis projects** (2007-2016+) in areas including:

- Software visualization and 3D graphics
- Graph layout algorithms
- Augmented reality applications
- Interactive data visualization
- Software metrics and evolution analysis
- Multimedia content visualization

Recent thesis topics include:
- Photorealistic data visualization in augmented reality
- Graph clustering and edge bundling algorithms
- Program execution visualization
- Dynamic object-oriented program animation
- Interactive 3D browsing systems

For more details, see the [Supervised Theses](posts.qmd) section.

---

## Collaborations

I am actively seeking collaborations in:
- Software engineering visualization
- Data visualization and visual analytics
- 3D graphics and augmented reality
- Graph algorithms and network visualization

If you're interested in collaboration or have research questions, please [get in touch](contact.qmd).
